frameworks.name: interoceptive / visceral-afferent shaping of brain dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Heart and gut intrinsic signals continuously modulate ongoing brain activity.

frameworks.name: resting-state networks
frameworks.one_line: Canonical RSNs are complemented by visceral-input-driven dynamics.

frameworks.name: embodied cognition / mind-body integration
frameworks.one_line: Frames visceral inputs as blurring cognition, emotion, and consciousness.

core_question: How do intrinsic electrical signals from the heart and gastrointestinal tract shape ongoing brain dynamics and cognition?
key_assumptions: Heart and GI tract generate intrinsic electrical activity independently of brain command
Ascending visceral signals reach the brain continuously
Brain dynamics at rest are not fully captured by canonical RSNs alone
key_findings: Ascending cardiac and GI signals actively shape resting brain dynamics
Cardiac signals modulate processing of external sensory information
Cardiac signals influence spontaneous, internal cognition
interpretations: Visceral inputs complement canonical resting-state networks rather than replacing them
Findings blur boundaries between cognition, emotion, consciousness, and mind-body
